IRDM.headline: Why Viasat Stock Went to the Moon Today
IRDM.description: Rocket Lab announced an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications, which sparked investor enthusiasm across the space sector. Viasat stock surged 19.74% despite not being directly involved in the deal, as investors speculate it could become a future merger target due to its valuable wireless spectrum holdings. The acquisition signals industry consolidation as smaller space companies seek to compete with SpaceX.

IRDM.headline: Why Rocket Lab Stock Went to the Moon Today
IRDM.description: Rocket Lab announced an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications, paying $54 per share ($27 cash, rest in stock). The deal combines Rocket Lab's launch capabilities with Iridium's satellite constellation for IoT, aviation, maritime, and positioning services, positioning the company to compete more directly with SpaceX's Starlink business.

VSAT.headline: Why Viasat Stock Went to the Moon Today

ASTS.headline: SpaceX Has Real Value—But These 3 Stocks Have Better Odds Right Now
ASTS.description: While SpaceX's $1.3-1.5 trillion valuation is justified by its three business segments (launch, Starlink, and xAI compute), analysts suggest better investment opportunities exist. ASML, Northrop Grumman, and GE Vernova offer more attractive risk-reward profiles in AI infrastructure, space economy, and power generation respectively. Tesla and AST SpaceMobile are viewed as overvalued relative to their growth prospects.

ASTS.headline: Buy AST SpaceMobile Before Aug. 1 Due to This Opportunity
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile is preparing to launch three BlueBird satellites in August 2026 with larger 2,400-square-foot antennas expected to nearly double current download speeds to ~200 Mbps. The company targets 45 satellites in orbit by end of 2026 and projects revenue growth from $14.7M in Q1 to $150-200M for full year 2026, with potential $1B revenue in 2027. With $3.5B in cash and diversified launch partnerships, the upcoming launches represent a key test of the company's ability to scale its satelli

CBRS.headline: Why Cerebras Stock Is Skyrocketing Today
CBRS.description: Cerebras stock surged over 20% this week following a 29% plunge the previous week, driven by Ark Invest's purchase of shares across two of its ETFs. The semiconductor company, which designs AI chips, received no company-specific news but benefited from Cathie Wood's investment endorsement. However, the article cautions investors against blindly following other investors' activity given the stock's significant volatility.

CBRS.headline: Where Will Cerebras Stock be in 3 Years?
CBRS.description: Cerebras Systems, an AI chip company, went public in May 2026 and has experienced significant volatility, losing 41% since its IPO pop. Despite disappointing Q1 2026 earnings guidance and margin concerns, analysts remain bullish with a 12-month price target of $300 (65% upside). The company's wafer-sized chips and major partnerships with OpenAI ($20B+) and Amazon position it for potential 175% upside over three years if it achieves $7.4B revenue by 2028, though its current 65x price-to-sales val

CBRS.headline: OpenAI Reportedly Considers Delaying Its IPO. Should You Worry About AI Stocks?
CBRS.description: OpenAI is reportedly considering delaying its IPO until next year to potentially achieve a $1 trillion valuation, according to The New York Times. While AI stocks have faced some pressure due to valuation concerns and high spending, the article argues investors shouldn't worry if they hold quality AI companies with strong competitive positions, as the AI growth story is likely still in early stages.

ALAB.headline: The Data Center Build-Out Is Accelerating. Here Are 3 Stocks to Buy Beyond Chips.
ALAB.description: As AI data center investment accelerates, opportunities extend beyond chip makers to networking, cloud infrastructure, and power management suppliers. Astera Labs, CoreWeave, and Vertiv are positioned to benefit from growing demand for high-speed networking, GPU cloud services, and power/cooling systems in AI data centers.

PL.headline: After the SpaceX IPO, Investors Are Looking Past Launch — to the Companies That Sell What Satellites See
PL.description: Following SpaceX's IPO, investors are increasingly focusing on the Earth-observation data layer of the space economy. Companies like Planet Labs, which sells daily satellite imagery and analytics, are benefiting from cheaper launch costs and demonstrating strong financial growth. The sector includes other players like Satellogic and BlackSky, each with distinct business models in the geospatial intelligence market.

MU.headline: Is OpenAI’s IPO Delay a Warning for AI Investors?
MU.description: OpenAI is reportedly delaying its IPO from late 2026 to 2027, raising concerns about AI valuations and market appetite. SpaceX's recent IPO saw significant share decline after announcing a major AI acquisition, signaling potential investor hesitation. Chipmakers, which power AI infrastructure, have experienced recent selloffs, though strong earnings from Micron suggest the sector remains fundamentally sound. Investors should monitor hardware and infrastructure providers as proxies for AI market

MU.headline: Why Sandisk Stock Just Dropped
MU.description: Sandisk stock fell 4% Monday despite Jefferies doubling its price target to $3,000 on Friday. The decline was triggered by reports that Apple is seeking permission to buy memory chips from Chinese supplier CXMT instead of Micron and Sandisk. While CXMT primarily supplies DRAM rather than NAND flash, investors worry Chinese competitors could threaten Sandisk's market share. However, analysts note that with memory supply deficits expected through 2027-2028 and long-term supply deals being locked i

MU.headline: Micron Technology Stock: Is It Heading for a $2 Trillion Valuation?
MU.description: Micron Technology's stock has surged 800% over the past 12 months, reaching a $1.3 trillion market cap, driven by strong demand and limited supply of memory and storage products. While the company trades at an attractive forward P/E of 7 compared to the S&P 500 average of 21, reaching a $2 trillion valuation would require a 56% increase. However, the author recommends caution due to the industry's cyclical nature and potential for significant correction once supply catches up with demand.

MU.headline: SK Hynix Just Sent a Huge Warning to Micron Investors
MU.description: SK Hynix is raising $29 billion through a Nasdaq listing to fund aggressive expansion of memory chip production capacity through 2030. This capital-backed expansion poses a competitive threat to Micron, as increased supply from SK Hynix could pressure chip prices and accelerate the peak of the current memory chip earnings cycle. Micron is currently trading at relatively expensive valuations (9x forward earnings) and faces risks if competition accelerates the downturn timeline.

MU.headline: How Buying Penguin Solutions Today Could 10X Your Net Worth
MU.description: Penguin Solutions, a memory and AI infrastructure company, shows promise despite a 6% year-over-year revenue decline. The decline is attributed to legacy business slowdown, while its high-growth integrated memory segment surged 63% YoY, driven by AI and high-performance computing customer deals. The company recently received Nvidia's AI factory partner designation, positioning it for potential significant growth.

MU.headline: MEXC Launches TradFi Gala With 1,000,000 USDT Reward Pool
MU.description: MEXC has launched the TradFi Gala, a month-long Futures trading campaign offering 1,000,000 USDT in rewards with 0-fee trading on selected pairs. The campaign reflects surging demand for AI infrastructure and semiconductor stocks, with MEXC's May Stock Futures volume increasing 105% month-over-month and Micron (MU) Futures volume surging 1,002%.

MU.headline: AI’s Hidden Cost Problem Is Making Memory Chip Makers the Biggest Winners
MU.description: As AI infrastructure buildout accelerates, memory chip suppliers (Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix) have become critical bottlenecks, commanding pricing power similar to oil producers during an airline boom. While hyperscalers absorb rising memory costs to maintain competitive advantage, the economics remain unsustainable long-term. The AI profit pool is fragmenting, with memory suppliers capturing disproportionate gains until efficiency improvements, better monetization, or new supply capacity arrives

MU.headline: Is Micron Stock the New Nvidia?
MU.description: Micron Technology has emerged as a major AI beneficiary, providing essential memory and storage products for AI systems. With revenue quadrupling to over $41 billion and net income reaching $28 billion in the latest quarter, Micron's stock has surged over 800% in the past year. While the company faces competition from SK Hynix in AI memory, strong demand exceeding supply and high gross margins (84%) position Micron similarly to Nvidia's growth trajectory, though with potentially more risk.

MU.headline: The AI Memory Supercycle Could Last Longer Than Investors Expect. That's Great News for Micron and Sandisk.
MU.description: Micron's Q3 FY2026 earnings significantly exceeded expectations with $41.5B in revenue versus $33.5B guidance, suggesting the AI-driven memory chip supercycle is still in early stages and likely to extend longer than anticipated. The company's record-level investments and multiyear customer agreements indicate sustained demand, while Sandisk is expected to report similarly strong results. These developments suggest the memory supercycle will maintain elevated prices and high margins for extended

MU.headline: Micron Just Revealed a Massive Multi-Decade Growth Opportunity. It's Not AI Data Centers. It Might Be Even Bigger.
MU.description: Micron's CEO highlighted humanoid robots and autonomous vehicles as a massive multi-decade growth opportunity for memory and storage demand. Humanoid robots require 10 times more memory than advanced autonomous vehicles. Bank of America predicts 300 million robots globally by 2040 and 3 billion by 2060, potentially creating sustained demand that could reduce Micron's cyclical nature.
